title: AbortController
body:
## Definition

**`AbortController`** provides an **`AbortSignal`** to cancel async operations like `fetch`, event listeners, and some APIs cooperatively.

## Why it exists

Cancel in-flight requests when users navigate away, debounce searches, or abort stale responses.

## How it works

```js
const controller = new AbortController();
fetch("/api", { signal: controller.signal });
controller.abort(); // rejects fetch with AbortError
```

Pass `signal` to supported APIs; listen with `signal.addEventListener("abort", ...)`.

## Common confusion

Abort is cooperative — code must respect the signal. Aborted [Promises](concept:promise) reject; handle errors explicitly.

## In practice

Standard for cancellable `fetch` in React effects cleanup and [TanStack Query](concept:tanstack-query) cancellation.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[AbortController](concept:abort-controller)** provides an **[AbortSignal](concept:abort-controller)** to cancel [async](concept:async-await) operations like [fetch](concept:fetch), event listeners, and [some](concept:array-some-every) APIs cooperatively.

## Why it exists

Cancel in-flight requests when users navigate away, [debounce](concept:debounce-throttle) searches, or abort stale responses.

## How it works

```js
const controller = new AbortController();
fetch("/api", { signal: controller.signal });
controller.abort(); // rejects fetch with AbortError
```

Pass `signal` to supported APIs; listen with `signal.addEventListener("abort", ...)`.

## Common confusion

Abort is cooperative — code must respect the signal. Aborted [Promises](concept:promise) reject; handle errors explicitly.

## In practice

Standard for cancellable [fetch](concept:fetch) in React effects cleanup and [TanStack Query](concept:tanstack-query) cancellation.
